title: Resource
description:
A Resource is a named entity of the business — anything the system tracks, manages, operates on, or involves in work.

Resources do not declare a 'type'. Their semantic role is inferred from how they are referenced:
- A Resource referenced as an Operation target is a target Resource (e.g. Loan).
- A Resource referenced from `Membership.role` or `Task.actor` is acting as a Role (e.g. Underwriter, Father).
- A Resource that has `memberships[]` is acting as a User (e.g. JoeKleier).
- A Resource referenced from `Membership.in` or another Resource's `scope` is acting as a Group (e.g. Family, Account).

The same Resource can play multiple semantic roles simultaneously. Customer is both a tracked entity and an actor; Account is both a tracked entity, an actor (the company acts), and a Group (employees scoped within it). No multi-flag declaration is needed.

Roles, Users, and Groups are all Resources — there is no separate primitive for any of them.

name.description: The singular PascalCase name of the resource.

description.description: A human-readable explanation of what this resource represents in the domain.

domain.description: The dot-separated domain path this resource belongs to.

attributes.description: The attributes (fields) that describe this resource.

actions.description: The actions that can be performed on this resource.

parent.description: Optional parent Resource name — used when this Resource is acting as a Role and participates in a hierarchy (org-chart or permission composition). E.g. SeniorUnderwriter.parent = Underwriter; ClosingSpecialist.parent = LendingManager.

scope.description: Optional Resource name naming the Group this Resource must be exercised within when acting as a Role. E.g. Father.scope = Family; Underwriter.scope = BankDepartment. A Role with no scope is global. The validator enforces that any Membership referencing this Role uses an `in` matching this scope.

memberships.description: When this Resource acts as a User, the (role, group) pairs it embodies. Each entry pins a Role-Resource within a specific Group-Resource. E.g. JoeKleier holds [{role: Father, in: Family}, {role: Husband, in: Marriage}].

in.description: The Group-Resource the role is exercised within (PascalCase Resource name). Must match the role's `scope` when declared.

description.description: Optional human-readable note about this membership.

examples.description: Representative example records for this resource. Used as stub data when the API is unavailable.

examples.name: Loan
examples.description: A financial loan issued to a borrower with repayment terms.

examples.name: Family
examples.description: A household unit. Acts as a Group scoping family-role memberships.

examples.name: JoeKleier
examples.description: Named individual; documentation roster entry, not auth identity.
